sanskrit: देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम्। ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते॥
transliteration: deva-dvija-guru-prājña-pūjanaṁ śaucam ārjavam | brahmacaryam ahiṁsā ca śārīraṁ tapa ucyate ||
literal_meaning: Worship of the Supreme Lord, twice-born, teacher and the wise; cleanliness, straightforwardness, celibacy and non-violence—this is declared bodily austerity.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists six embodied disciplines that train the nervous system to channel instinctual energy toward prosocial aims: reverence, hygiene, authenticity, sexual self-containment, and harmlessness. They are not moralistic rules but somatic ‘weights’ that strengthen the pre-frontal ‘muscle’ to hold tension without acting out or repressing it.
psychological_state: The practitioner is learning to convert raw drives into socially constructive energy; body becomes a laboratory for impulse-delay and value-based action.
trigger_scenario: You feel the urge to scroll porn or binge junk-food after a stressful Zoom day; instead you take a cold shower, tidy the room, and text gratitude to a mentor—your body just enacted the verse.
anchor_text: Train your body through reverence, cleanliness, honesty, self-restraint, and harmlessness to steady the mind.
